# Trust anchors for the in-kernel TLS client

These C files are `br_x509_trust_anchor[]` tables generated by the official BearSSL
`brssl ta` tool (BearSSL 7bea48e, hosted build) and embedded into the kernel's HTTPS
runtime (`kernel/drivers/virtio/https_get_runtime.c`). Each defines `TAs[]` + `TAs_NUM`;
the runtime `#include`s exactly one (`-DTLS_GOOGLE` selects `google_ta.c`, otherwise
`local_ta.c`).

## local_ta.c — the deterministic CI gate (`tools/tls/https-get-test.sh`)
Trust anchor = the committed self-signed certificate `host_test.pem` (CN=`host.test`,
SAN `DNS:host.test`), generated with:

    openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout host_test.key -out host_test.pem \
        -days 3650 -nodes -subj "/CN=host.test" -addext "subjectAltName=DNS:host.test"
    brssl ta host_test.pem > local_ta.c

A self-signed cert is its own trust anchor. The local python HTTPS test server uses
`host_test.pem`/`host_test.key`, so BearSSL validates the exact cert it serves. The key
is a throwaway used only by the test harness — committing it is intentional and safe.

## google_ta.c — the best-effort real fetch (`tools/tls/google-https-test.sh`)
Trust anchor = **GTS Root R1** (`C=US, O=Google Trust Services LLC, CN=GTS Root R1`),
SHA-256 fingerprint
`3E:E0:27:8D:F7:1F:A3:C1:25:C4:CD:48:7F:01:D7:74:69:4E:6F:C5:7E:0C:D9:4C:24:EF:D7:69:13:39:18:E5`.

It was extracted from google.com's live chain (the chain presents
`*.google.com <- WR2 <- GTS Root R1 <- GlobalSign Root CA`; the third cert IS GTS Root R1)
and converted:

    openssl s_client -connect google.com:443 -servername google.com -showcerts </dev/null
    # save the GTS Root R1 block as gts_root_r1.pem
    brssl ta gts_root_r1.pem > google_ta.c

BearSSL anchors on the subject DN + public key, so this validates Google's real chain:
the server-sent WR2 intermediate chains up to GTS Root R1, which we trust as the anchor.
